items.title: Transgender people join LGBTQ+ pilgrimage to Vatican
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A group of transgender people took part in the LGBTQ+ jubilee at the Vatican Saturday, joining the crowd walking down Via Della Conciliazione and passing through the Holy Door. (AP video and production by Trisha Thomas)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Tourists in Lisbon undeterred by streetcar crash which killed 16 people
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The yellow and white streetcar has been part of the city’s identity since the turn of the last century, when the first electric cars entered service in 1901. 

(AP video and production by Hernan Munoz)

items.title: Destruction and despair after deadly Israeli strike on refugee camp
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The strike was one of several on northern Gaza, where the Israeli military says it will expand its military operations against Hamas in Gaza City.

items.title: Activists rally in support of group designated as terrorist by UK government
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Police on Saturday arrested some of the hundreds of protesters who gathered outside Parliament in London to defy a ban on the group Palestine Action, which has been deemed a terrorist organization by the government.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Hindus bid traditional farewell to elephant-headed Hindu God Ganesha at festival
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A massive crowd of devotees danced and followed giant-sized Ganesha idols which were paraded through the streets on Saturday before their ritual immersion in the sea that symbolizes sending the god back to his mythical home.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Nepal's festival season starts with parade for living goddess
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The weeklong Indra Jatra festival is the first of many other coming celebrations in the predominantly Hindu nation, which include Dasain, the main festival, celebrated later this month, and Tihar, or Diwali, the festival of lights, in October. (AP video by Upendra Man Singh)


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Hundreds pay last respects to Italian fashion icon Giorgio Armani
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Armani died Thursday at 91 at his home in central Milan surrounded by loved ones. His fashion house said he worked up to the end. One of his final projects was a runway show marking 50 years of his signature Giorgio Armani brand, which is due to close Milan Fashion Week later this month.

(AP video by Brian Hendrie)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Street artist driven by world events leaves tiny engravings in pavements and buildings across Paris
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Artist Théo Haggaï has been collecting pieces of broken wall and paving from Paris streets, engraving them with tiny designs and returning them to the exact spot where he found them -- then posting videos of the process online. 

(AP video shot by Nicolas Garriga)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Overburdened with public and private school students, Ohio school district buses struggle
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A scramble is underway for some Ohio families over a staple of the back-to-school season: rides on the big, yellow school bus. (AP Video by Patrick Aftoora-Orsagos)

items.title: How romance and a road trip led to the purchase of the world's oldest drive-in theater
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In a romance and adventure worthy of the big screen, a Pennsylvania couple is preserving the past and forging a future as the owners of the world’s oldest drive-in movie theater. (AP video: Mingson Lau and Gene Puskar)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: 'Hero rats' in Tanzania detect land mines and tuberculosis
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>They're known as 'hero rats' and for good reason.

In Tanzania, African giant pouched rats are being trained to sniff out landmines, diseases and even humans buried under rubble. They have a highly sensitive sense of smell, which makes them invaluable during lifesaving missions.

#shorts #tanzania #rats #africa</article>

items.title: Palestinians mourn latest dead ahead of Israeli operation to seize control of Gaza City
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 27 people were killed in the latest Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip, according to hospital officials. In Gaza City, children combed through the debris of shelters destroyed in the strikes.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Displaced Bedouin families in limbo as Syrian government and Druze authorities remain at odds
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Bedouin families evacuated their villages during sectarian fighting more than a month ago in neighboring Sweida province. Since then, the central government in Damascus has been in a standoff with local Druze authorities in Sweida, while the displaced have been left in a state of limbo.

(AP Video shot by Ghait Alsayed, produced by Malak Harb)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Shark fatally mauls man off beach near Sydney
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A 57-year-old man died in Sydney on Saturday after being attacked by what police believe to be a shark in the city’s northern beaches.

items.title: New Delhi family seeks relief from some of the worst floods to hit northern India in decades
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Bindu Pandey left her family’s belongings and sought shelter in a relief tent after floodwaters of the Yamuna River gushed into her home in New Delhi.

AP Video shot by Piyush Nagpal
